Reports About Source

vigilant

Independent researcher. Crypto and Web3 protocols read under adversarial assumptions — what survives when the operator is hostile and only the math is left. Hardware and blockchain background.

Reports

Long-form analyst reports. Each follows a common methodology — competitor mapping, technical-architecture analysis, economic and game-theoretic analysis, product specification, and a long-form thesis with explicit falsifiability.

Fileverse

Data cutoff · 2026-04-27

End-to-end encrypted productivity suite combining a local-first document editor, an onchain-aware spreadsheet, and multi-provider IPFS persistence with an Ethereum and Gnosis Chain content registry. Analyst report covers mechanism, comparative landscape against CryptPad and Proton, economic and game-theoretic properties under a no-token operating model, and observable conditions that would change our view.

Read report → Source files

About

Reports are produced via a five-phase analytical pipeline: competitor mapping, parallel teardown across competitive, technical, and economic dimensions, integrated product specification, methodology and disclosures, and a long-form thesis report. Every quantitative claim carries an inline citation at point of claim. Forward-looking statements use explicit conditional framing.

Each report includes a methodology page documenting sources consulted, frameworks applied, limitations, and disclosures (including data cutoff and any positions held). Reports also include a "What would change our view" subsection that names observable, falsifiable conditions under which the thesis would be revised.

The voice is balanced analyst — the reports present multiple sides of contested questions and let the reader form a view, rather than arguing a single thesis.
